prompt:
Release engineering has isolated a reproducibility regression in the Beacon C library package. Two clean builds from identical source produce byte-different `libbeacon.a` files whenever compiler completion order or object mtimes differ between runners. That defeats artifact attestation even though the compiled object payloads are identical.

Repair the static-library archive step so it has one canonical result for a given set of object bytes. Archive members must be ordered by object filename in C byte order, and each member's timestamp, owner ID, and group ID must be zero rather than copied from the build worker. This is an archive-only repair: do not change compiler inputs or flags, object contents, the three exported `beacon_*` symbols, the public header, the library name, or the install layout (`$DESTDIR$PREFIX/lib/libbeacon.a` and `$DESTDIR$PREFIX/include/beacon.h`). Keep honoring the Makefile's `AR` selection.

Run `python3 tests/test_reproducible.py` from the repository root. The suite is offline, assigns fixed mtimes instead of sleeping, performs two independent compilations, inspects the archive headers and members, and checks installation. The Makefile, source/header fixtures, ignore file, and test are protected; leave them unchanged.

full_distill.primary_skill: make a C static-library build reproducible by canonicalizing archive member order and metadata without altering compilation or packaging contracts
full_distill.baseline_evidence: the protected verifier builds identical object payloads under two fixed completion-time layouts, then fails because libbeacon.a preserves different member order and timestamps
full_distill.success_contract: both builds produce byte-identical deterministic archives with lexical members and zeroed member time/owner fields while object hashes, exported symbols, AR selection, and install paths remain unchanged
adversarial_factors: object files are compiled independently and assigned opposing fixed mtimes, so relying on discovery or completion order remains observable without wall-clock sleeps
the suite parses ar headers directly as well as comparing bytes, so copying a previously built artifact or normalizing only the outer file timestamp is insufficient
member payloads are compared with the compiled objects and the protected sources and flags cannot be edited, preventing recompilation tricks from satisfying reproducibility
the expected global symbols and exact DESTDIR/PREFIX layout are checked after the archive repair, catching ABI or packaging drift
the Makefile passes its configurable AR value through the helper, so bypassing the declared tool contract is outside scope
